title: Deliciously Crumbly Uruguayan Cheese Empanadas
description: A kid-friendly Uruguayan dish featuring fermented cheese and ginger cordial, perfect for a Mediterranean-inspired snack. This recipe uses a fondue pot for a fun and interactive eating experience.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the flour, salt, and baking powder.
Add the grated cheese, ginger cordial, eggs, milk, and melted butter to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle and place a tablespoon of cheese in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the cheese and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Place the empanadas in a fondue pot and bake for 20 minutes or until golden brown.
Serve the empanadas hot and enjoy.
Let the empanadas cool for a few minutes before serving to kids.
